title: Building Great Agent Skills: The Missing Manual
summary: A framework for building high-quality AI agent skills by addressing "skill hell" through structured design. The approach covers five key dimensions: Trigger (user-invoked vs model-invoked), Structure (steps and reference organization), Steering (leading words to guide agent behavior), increased task complexity per step, and Pruning (removing legacy material and no-ops).
writeup: This talk lays out a framework for building high-quality AI agent skills, framing the problem as "skill hell" that structured design can address. It breaks the work into five dimensions: Trigger (user-invoked versus model-invoked), Structure (organizing steps and reference material), Steering (leading words that guide agent behavior), increasing task complexity per step, and Pruning (cutting legacy material and no-ops). The takeaway is a repeatable design vocabulary for skill authoring, connecting prompt engineering and token optimization to concrete choices about how skills are triggered, organized, and trimmed.
